text: The surgeon divides the round ligament and opens the falciform and anterior coronary ligaments. Using the second bipolar forceps, the robotic arm number four pushes down the liver, freeing the anterior face of the right hepatic vein.

The surgeon divides the round ligament and opens the falciform and anterior coronary ligaments. The robotic arm number four uses bipolar forceps to push down the liver, freeing the anterior face of the right hepatic vein. The gallbladder is dissected, and the cystic artery and duct are sectioned between two hemolog clips.

The surgeon dissects the gallbladder, sections the cystic artery and duct between two hemolog clips, and pushes the cystic stump to the left. This maneuver exposes the right hepatic artery, which is then controlled and suctioned between clips.

The assistant pushes the cystic stump to the left, exposing the right hepatic artery, which is controlled and clipped. The same maneuver reveals the portal vein, allowing identification of the left portal vein and control of the right portal vein, which is then secured with tape.
The portal vein is exposed by traction of the cystic stump to the left. The left portal vein is identified, and the right portal vein is controlled and secured with tape.
The surgeon uses wristed robotic instruments to place hemoclips on the right portal vein, which is then sectioned.

The surgeon uses wristed robotic instruments to place hemoclips easily. The right portal vein is sectioned, and the posterior face of the caudate lobe is exposed to release the inferior vena cava. Accessory hepatic veins are controlled and sectioned.

The posterior face of the caudate lobe is exposed, allowing the release of the inferior vena cava. Accessory hepatic veins are controlled and sectioned between clips. The demarcation line is marked with a monopolar hook and checked using an intravenous injection of endothelial green. Parenchymal transection is performed by the assistant using a CUSA and irrigation device.

The surgeon marks the demarcation line on the liver using a monopolar hook, verified with an intravenous injection of endothelial green. The assistant performs parenchymal transection using a CUSA and irrigation device, while the robotic surgeon manipulates two bipolar forceps and a monopolar hook. The irrigation system keeps the bipolar forceps clean and enhances their efficiency. The segment V hepatic vein is controlled at the right edge of the middle hepatic vein and sectioned. The right hepatic duct is sectioned and ligated with clips after parenchymal transection, which exposes the entire face of the inferior vena cava.

The robotic surgeon manipulates two robotic bipolars and a monopolar hook, using irrigation to keep the instruments clean and enhance bipolar efficiency. The segment V hepatic vein is controlled at the right edge of the middle hepatic vein and sectioned. Following parenchymal transection, the right hepatic duct is sectioned and ligated with clips to prevent hepatic biliary injury.

The parenchymal transection reveals the entire face of the inferior vena cava. The surgeon controls the Segment VIII hepatic vein, and sections it at the right edge of the middle hepatic vein using clips.

The surgeon controls the Segment VIII hepatic vein, performing an end section at the right edge of the middle hepatic vein. The anterior face of the inferior vena cava is completely freed, revealing its surface.
The anterior face of the inferior vena cava is fully exposed, revealing the voluminous right accessory vein. The surgeon controls and sections this vein using a vascular stapler.

The surgeon uses a vascular stapler to control and section the voluminous right accessory vein, followed by the right hepatic vein. The camera is repositioned on arm number three to enhance the view of the right hepatic vein during the procedure.

The surgeon controls and sections the right hepatic vein using a vascular stapler. The camera is repositioned to arm number three, providing an enhanced view of the right hepatic vein. The Makushi ligament is sectioned between clips.

The surgeon sections the Makushi ligament and divides the right hepatic coronary ligament, completing the mobilization of the liver. The specimen is placed into a retrieval bag for extraction through a suprapubic incision.
The surgeon divides the right hepatic coronary ligament, completing the mobilization of the liver. The resected specimen is placed into a retrieval bag for extraction through a suprapubic incision.
